extra.message: SQL injection via id parameter directly concatenated into SELECT query. User input from params['id'] is inserted directly into SQL string without parameterization, allowing attackers to inject arbitrary SQL code.

Path traversal vulnerability via path parameter. Although os.path.abspath() is used, there is no validation to prevent directory traversal attacks. An attacker can read arbitrary files on the system by using path traversal sequences like '../../../etc/passwd'.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

extra.message: OS command injection via domain parameter. The domain parameter is concatenated directly into a shell command passed to subprocess.run() with shell=True. This allows arbitrary command execution through shell metacharacters like '; cat /etc/passwd; #'.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command

extra.message: XPath injection via name parameter. User input from params['name'] is directly interpolated into an XPath expression without proper escaping. An attacker can inject XPath syntax to bypass authentication or extract unintended data.

cwe: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data used in a Command

extra.message: SQL injection via comment parameter. User input from params['comment'] is directly interpolated into an INSERT statement using string formatting without parameterization, allowing SQL injection attacks.

Insecure deserialization using pickle.loads(). The object parameter from user input is deserialized with pickle.loads(), which can execute arbitrary code. Pickle should never be used with untrusted input.

XXE (XML External Entity) injection vulnerability. The xml parameter is parsed by lxml.etree.XMLParser with load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, and no_network=False. This allows attackers to read arbitrary files or perform SSRF attacks through XXE exploitation.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: Remote code execution via code injection. The include parameter allows loading arbitrary local or remote files, which are then executed with exec(). This is a critical vulnerability allowing complete system compromise. An attacker can execute arbitrary Python code by providing a URL or file path.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: SQL injection via password parameter in login query. While the username parameter is sanitized with re.sub(r'[^\w]', ''), the password parameter is directly concatenated into the SQL query without any sanitization. This enables authentication bypass attacks.
Open redirect vulnerability via redir parameter. The redir parameter is directly used in an HTTP refresh meta tag without any validation. An attacker can redirect users to malicious websites.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site

extra.message: JSONP callback injection via callback parameter. The callback parameter is not properly escaped before being used in the JSONP response wrapper. This can lead to code injection through callback parameter manipulation.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code
